meta.description: Design specification for HHS // OPENDATA Medicaid claims data visualizations
meta.source: HHS Open Data - Medicaid Spending Dashboard, FY2019–FY2023
meta.purpose: Reproduce charts matching the exact visual style of the HHS OPENDATA Medicaid dashboard

global_design.aesthetic: US Federal government open-data style — minimal, clean, authoritative, data-forward with no decorative flourishes
global_design.tone: Institutional, neutral, informational — designed for transparency and public accountability rather than persuasion

color_palette.primary_chart_color_description: Warm amber/marigold orange — the single dominant color used for ALL chart elements (lines, bars, area fills)
color_palette.note: The entire dashboard is monochromatic — only one color (amber/orange) is used for data, with gray tones for structural elements. There is NO use of secondary colors, gradients, or multi-color schemes.

typography.font_family: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
typography.font_family_description: Clean sans-serif system font stack — appears to be a neutral grotesque typeface

line_chart.chart_subtitle: Total Medicaid payments by month, FY2019–FY2023 (Oct 2018 – Sep 2023)

line.curve_type: monotone or catmull-rom (smooth interpolation between points)
line.point_markers: none — no visible dots on data points

area_fill.description: No area fill under the line — the chart uses a bare line only

x_axis.label_format: FY{YY} (e.g., FY19, FY20, FY21, FY22, FY23)
x_axis.tick_placement: at fiscal year boundaries (October of each year)
x_axis.note: Federal fiscal years run October – September

annotation_line.description: A thin vertical reference line appears around the FY20/FY21 boundary, likely marking the COVID-19 pandemic onset or a policy change
annotation_line.label: none visible

tooltip.style: floating box near the hovered point, left-aligned to the data point

footer.text: Federal fiscal years run October – September.

data_characteristics.trend: Upward trend from ~$9B (FY19) to ~$17B (FY23) with a dip around early FY20 (COVID onset) followed by steep recovery and continued growth
data_characteristics.seasonality: Mild monthly fluctuations with occasional dips, general pattern of year-end spikes
data_characteristics.notable_dip: Around Apr 2020 ($8.5B) — lowest point, coinciding with early COVID-19 pandemic

horizontal_bar_chart_procedures.chart_title_card: none — title appears outside the chart area in the card header
horizontal_bar_chart_procedures.chart_subtitle: Highest-cost HCPCS procedure codes across all providers, FY2019–FY2023

category_labels.truncation: multi-line wrapping for long names (e.g., 'Community Support, per Diem' wraps to 2-3 lines)

y_axis.sort_order: descending by value — highest value at top

data_characteristics.distribution: Extremely top-heavy — #1 is roughly 2x #2, and the top 2 dwarf all others
data_characteristics.long_tail: Items #10-#20 are relatively similar in magnitude ($3-7B each)
data_characteristics.notable_gap: Massive gap between #1 (Personal Care ~$82B) and #2 (Clinic Visit ~$38B)

horizontal_bar_chart_procedures.variant_note: chart2-top-procedures.png appears to exclude 'Personal Care Services' (#1 item), starting from 'Clinic Visit/Encounter'. This may be an alternate view or scroll state.

category_labels.truncation: Ellipsis (...) for very long names (e.g., 'Los Angeles County Departme...', 'Alabama Department Of Menta...')

data_characteristics.distribution: Top 2 are close together, then a drop to #3, followed by a relatively gradual decline

horizontal_bar_chart_providers.variant_note: chart3-top-providers.png appears to exclude 'Public Partnerships LLC' (#1 item), starting from 'Los Angeles County Department...'. May be an alternate scroll or filtered view.

card_view.description: Charts are displayed inside card containers with headers, action links, and footers

header.title: bold, 18px, dark
header.subtitle: regular, 13px, medium gray
header.action_links: download icons for Image and Data, top-right

body.chart_area: full width of card, with left margin for labels

footer.note_text: small italic-style note (e.g., fiscal year definition)

standalone_chart_view.description: Charts displayed without card wrapper — just the chart with axis labels and branding

responsive_behavior.label_wrapping: long category names wrap to multiple lines rather than truncating
responsive_behavior.bar_spacing_adjusts: bar height and gap appear consistent regardless of count

design_principles.monochromatic: Single amber/orange color for ALL data elements — no secondary colors
design_principles.minimal_chrome: No borders on bars, no decorative elements, no shadows on chart elements
design_principles.whitespace: Generous whitespace between elements — charts breathe
design_principles.data_ink_ratio: Very high — almost all ink is data, minimal non-data ink
design_principles.gridlines: Light gray, horizontal only for bar charts, horizontal only for line charts — never vertical gridlines on line charts
design_principles.no_legends: No color legends needed since only one color is used
design_principles.sort_order: Always descending by value for bar charts — largest at top
design_principles.zero_baseline: All charts start at $0 — no misleading truncated axes
design_principles.government_data_viz_standards: Follows US government open data visualization conventions — clean, accessible, high contrast text on white

accessibility_notes.contrast_ratio: High — dark text on white background, amber bars have sufficient contrast
accessibility_notes.color_dependency: Low — single color means no reliance on color differentiation between categories
accessibility_notes.screen_reader: Data download links available (CSV/data export implied by 'Data' action link)

implementation_recommendations.charting_library: D3.js, Vega-Lite, Observable Plot, or Recharts — any library that supports clean minimal styling
implementation_recommendations.css_reset: Use white background, remove all default chart library chrome/decorations
implementation_recommendations.font_stack: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
implementation_recommendations.tooltip_library: Native SVG tooltip or lightweight library — dark background, white text
